title: Parameter set expansion — 7 size knobs → 16 recipe parameters covering proportions, posture, mounting offsets, and shape style
intent: S101's bench exposes 7 size knobs (headSize, torsoHeight, torsoWidth, armLength, armWidth, legLength, legWidth). That's the minimum-viable knob set — enough to prove the bench plumbing but not enough to produce a few thousand visibly distinct bombers. This story expands the recipe surface to 16 parameters covering: full proportions (4 axes × 2 buckets each), posture (forward tilt, arm rest angle), mounting offsets (where shoulders + hips attach on the torso), and shape style (which Three.js geometry class each part uses). Each parameter has a clamped range, a default, and a deterministic seed-derivation rule so a recipe with only `{ seed: 42 }` still produces a fully-specified bomber.
rationale: Source: `docs/game-design/characters-and-visual.md` §2.1 (the original 12-value recipe shape). The S101 v0 collapsed the recipe to 7 size scalars; this expansion restores the full design intent and adds posture + mounting + style parameters that were implicit in the prose but not in the schema. Without expanded parameters, the bench produces visually-clustered bombers — sliders move size only, never shape — and the 'a few thousand distinct silhouettes' variation budget in characters-and-visual.md §2.3 doesn't hold. Sized to land after GDP-2026-05-21-001 (multi-mesh) — the new parameters describe pivot positions and per-mesh geometry, which only make sense once the tree exists.
acceptanceHints: Depends on GDP-2026-05-21-001. The 16 parameters target the multi-mesh tree, not the merged-geometry v0.

Replace BomberMeshSpec with `CharacterRecipe` (the schema lives at examples/procbomber-bench/schemas/scene-extensions.schema.json, mirrors the docs/game-design/characters-and-visual.md §2.1 definition).
16 parameters, grouped (proportions / posture / mounting / style):
PROPORTIONS (6):
headSize        — 0.25..0.45 (default 0.35; was already in v0)
torsoHeight     — 0.35..0.60 (default 0.45)
torsoWidth      — 0.30..0.60 (default 0.45)
armLength       — 0.30..0.55 (default 0.40); spans BOTH upperArm + forearm equally for now
armWidth        — 0.10..0.22 (default 0.15)
legLength       — 0.25..0.50 (default 0.35); spans BOTH upperLeg + lowerLeg equally
legWidth        — 0.13..0.25 (default 0.18)
POSTURE (3):
forwardTilt     — -0.15..+0.15 rad (default 0); whole-torso pitch; positive leans forward
armRestAngle    — 0..0.4 rad (default 0.1); resting shoulder pitch; 0 = arms straight down, +0.4 = slightly outward
legStance       — 0..0.2 rad (default 0.05); resting hip outward yaw; 0 = knees parallel, +0.2 = wider stance
MOUNTING (3):
shoulderHeight  — 0..1 (default 0.85); fraction of torsoHeight where shoulders attach (1 = top, 0.85 = standard humanoid)
hipWidth        — 0.4..0.8 (default 0.5); fraction of torsoWidth where hips separate
neckHeight      — 0..0.2 (default 0.05); world-units of gap between torso-top and head-bottom
STYLE (3):
headShape       — enum 'box' | 'sphere' | 'cappedCylinder' (default 'box', matches v0)
torsoShape      — enum 'box' | 'capsule' | 'roundedBox' (default 'box')
limbShape       — enum 'box' | 'cylinder' | 'capsule' (default 'box'); applies to all 4 limbs (no per-limb override yet)
All parameters are optional in the recipe. Unspecified ones derive deterministically from the recipe's seed via xor-shift-32 mapping into the clamped range. Same seed → same defaults, every time.

Validator (CharacterRecipe schema): unknown fields rejected, out-of-range values rejected (no silent clamp), enum values strictly checked. Validation errors surface as 'recipe field X: value Y outside [min..max]' so an agent crafting a recipe via probe gets actionable feedback.

Bench UI: the existing 7-slider DOM overlay extends to 16 controls + 3 dropdowns. Group them in collapsible sections (PROPORTIONS / POSTURE / MOUNTING / STYLE) so the panel stays readable. Reroll button now randomises ALL 16 parameters from a fresh seed, not just the size ones.

URL surface: `?recipe=<base64-json>` works for the full recipe. Per-field URL shortcuts (`?seed=42`, `?headShape=sphere`) stay as convenience knobs — last-write-wins, recipe overrides individual fields.
Determinism test: 200 random seeds → 200 calls to generateBomberMeshTree → no NaN, no out-of-range values, every parameter falls inside its declared range. Same seed twice → bit-identical mesh tree.

Variation test: 8 random seeds rendered side-by-side in the bench → visibly distinct silhouettes (the test is a manual QA step, not automated, but the bench README documents the expected visual diversity bar).

Performance: parameter expansion adds NO new draw calls (still 10 meshes per bomber). The cost is a slightly larger recipe JSON (~600 bytes vs ~200 for v0) and a slightly more complex builder. Both negligible per the visual-style.md §11 budget.

Out of scope (explicit): per-limb proportion overrides (left arm longer than right), per-finger control, facial proportions, accessory parameters (those land in GDP-2026-05-21-005). The 'humanoid' archetype is the only one this story handles — drone / puffball are deferred per characters-and-visual.md §6.1.
